mnemonics: APIE: The four pillars of OOP - Abstraction, Polymorphism, Inheritance, Encapsulation
keyPoints: OOP models software as a collection of objects that contain both data (attributes) and behavior (methods).
Procedural programming focuses on writing procedures or methods that perform operations on data, while OOP focuses on objects containing both.
The two main paradigms are Procedural (code as a sequence of steps) and Object-Oriented (code as interacting objects).
commonMistakes: Confusing classes and objects (a class is a blueprint, an object is an instance).
Using inheritance for simple code sharing instead of modeling an 'is-a' relationship, leading to fragile design.
